steps.title: Compare the two findings
steps.content: Text 1 says polar bear populations affected by sea-ice loss are at great risk of extinction. Text 2 describes Svalbard polar bears facing rapid sea-ice decline but remaining stable and well nourished after adding reindeer meat and birds' eggs to their diet.

steps.title: Focus on adaptation
steps.content: B disputes rates of sea-ice melt, which Text 2 does not challenge. C wrongly treats Svalbard bears as unaffected by loss of seal-hunting habitat. D says the issue is a single seal-hunting strategy, but Text 2's key point is dietary adaptation. The researchers would say Text 1 overlooks possible adaptation. The answer is A.
